subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV 2025, World Bank, WJP, RSF, TI, V-Dem, HRW, OCHA) + Nigerian official (NBS, Central Bank of Nigeria, NUPRC) + Nigerian independent press (Premium Times, BusinessDay, Vanguard, Guardian NG, Nairametrics, ICIR).

A painful reform cycle is underway: the 2023 naira float and fuel-subsidy removal drove an inflation/cost-of-living shock now easing, with a concluded bank recapitalisation, reserves at a multi-year high, and a major new refinery online.

Standout drags: severe internal insecurity, a power/infrastructure crisis, very high crime, weak healthcare, and a resource-curse oil model. Source-lean: HIGH but low-distortion Anglophone (English is official); northern-language insecurity reporting under-read.

economic.notes: A painful reform turn (float, subsidy removal, bank recapitalisation), against a crushing debt-service burden, high inflation, and a resource-curse oil model.

fiscal_state.notes: Debt ~50% GDP but a crushing debt-service-to-revenue ratio and weak revenue mobilisation; subsidy removal is painfully improving the trajectory. (IMF 2025 Art IV)

monetary_independence.notes: A reform-era central bank rebuilding credibility after the 2023 float, holding an orthodox stance from an intervention-heavy base. (CBN; IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The naira is a non-reserve floating currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Eased to ~15.7% after a 30%+ post-float peak and a 2024 CPI rebasing, but still a real cost-of-living crisis. (NBS; CBN)

banking_stability.notes: A major recapitalisation concluded in early 2026 lifting liquidity, against heavy oil-and-gas loan exposure. (CBN)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: A classic resource curse - oil output chronically below quota on theft and underinvestment - though a major new refinery and nascent non-oil/tech diversification are real positives. (NUPRC; NBS; BusinessDay)

institutional.notes: An improving statistics agency and a vibrant press, against entrenched corruption and weak delivery.

rule_of_law.notes: Entrenched corruption, slow courts, and impunity. (WJP 2025; TI)

statistical_integrity.notes: The statistics agency rebased GDP and CPI - credible and improving, but capacity-limited. (NBS; IMF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Patronage-ridden, with weak delivery. (IMF; World Bank)

press_freedom.notes: A vibrant but harassed press, with cybercrime-law prosecutions and a dangerous environment for journalists. (RSF; HRW 2026)

political_social.notes: A potential demographic dividend, against reform-hardship strain, deep cleavages, and limited belonging.

political_stability.notes: A 2023-elected government pushing painful reforms amid hardship protests; democratic but strained, with a 2027 election ahead. (Africanews; Guardian NG)

civil_liberties.notes: Formal freedoms undercut by crackdowns and a police-brutality legacy. (V-Dem; HRW)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Limited routes; not a relocation-destination regime. (observed)

social_cohesion.notes: Deep Muslim-north/Christian-south and ethnic/regional cleavages, with separatist undercurrents. (HRW)

demographics.notes: A huge, fast-growing young population - a jobs/Malthusian strain now, a potential dividend later. (World Bank; NBS)

geopolitical.notes: A non-aligned regional power, dominated by severe internal insecurity.

alliance_reliability.notes: A non-aligned regional power and ECOWAS leader, with the bloc weakened by Sahel coups and withdrawals. (The Conversation; BusinessDay)

conflict_involvement.notes: Severe internal insecurity - jihadist insurgency in the northeast, banditry and mass kidnapping in the northwest, and farmer-herder and separatist violence. (HRW 2026; SBM Intelligence)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: An FX-control legacy easing post-float; not sanctioned, with reserves at a multi-year high. (CBN; IMF)

physical_practical.notes: A power and infrastructure crisis, weak healthcare, high crime, and severe flood exposure.

climate.notes: Devastating recurrent floods, northern desertification, and Niger Delta degradation. (OCHA; UN News)

healthcare.notes: Weak, with low public spend, high out-of-pocket costs, and heavy doctor emigration. (Punch; Vanguard)

infrastructure.notes: A chronic power-supply crisis (repeated grid collapses) and poor roads - a major drag. (ICIR; Vanguard)

crime_safety.notes: High violent crime and endemic kidnapping-for-ransom. (SBM Intelligence; HRW)

disaster_insurance.notes: Very low insurance penetration against high flood/disaster exposure. (industry)

summary: A large, young, fast-growing economy mid-way through a painful but real reform cycle - a naira float, fuel-subsidy removal, a concluded bank recapitalisation, reserves at a multi-year high, and a major new refinery - with inflation easing from a post-reform shock. The profile is nonetheless dominated by drags: a crushing debt-service burden and a resource-curse oil model, entrenched corruption and weak delivery, a chronic power and infrastructure crisis, weak healthcare with heavy doctor emigration, very high violent crime and kidnapping, severe flooding, and severe internal insecurity from jihadist, bandit, and separatist violence.
